    10→
    11→class RFPSource(str, Enum):
    12→    """Enumeration of RFP data sources."""
    13→
    14→    GOVTRIBE = "govtribe"
    15→    OPENGOV = "opengov"
    16→    BIDNET = "bidnet"
    17→    MANUAL = "manual"
    18→
    19→
    20→class RFPStatus(str, Enum):
    21→    """Enumeration of RFP processing status."""
    22→
    23→    DISCOVERED = "discovered"
    24→    FILTERED = "filtered"
    25→    CLASSIFIED = "classified"
    26→    STORED = "stored"
    27→    PROPOSAL_GENERATED = "proposal_generated"
    28→    NOTIFIED = "notified"
    29→    SKIPPED = "skipped"
    30→    ERROR = "error"
    31→
    32→
    33→class RFPTag(str, Enum):
    34→    """Enumeration of RFP classification tags."""
    35→
    36→    AI = "AI"
    37→    DYNAMICS = "Dynamics"
    38→    MODERNIZATION = "Modernization"
    39→    CLOUD = "Cloud"
    40→    SECURITY = "Security"
    41→    DATA = "Data"
    42→    AUTOMATION = "Automation"
    43→    OTHER = "Other"
    44→
    45→
    46→class RFP(BaseModel):
    47→    """Model representing a Request for Proposal."""
    48→
    49→    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    50→    title: str = Field(..., description="RFP title")
    51→    description: str = Field(default="", description="RFP description or summary")
    52→    agency: str = Field(default="", description="Issuing agency or organization")
    53→    source: RFPSource = Field(default=RFPSource.MANUAL, description="Source portal")
    54→    source_url: str = Field(default="", description="Original URL of the RFP listing")
    55→
    56→    # Dates
    57→    posted_date: Optional[datetime] = Field(
    58→        default=None, description="Date the RFP was posted"
    59→    )
    60→    due_date: Optional[datetime] = Field(
    61→        default=None, description="Proposal submission deadline"
    62→    )
    63→    discovered_at: datetime = Field(
    64→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="When we discovered this RFP"
    65→    )
    66→
    67→    # Geography
    68→    location: str = Field(default="", description="Geographic location or region")
    69→    country: str = Field(default="US", description="Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)")
    70→    state: str = Field(default="", description="State or region code")
    71→
    72→    # Documents
    73→    pdf_url: Optional[str] = Field(
    74→        default=None, description="URL to the primary RFP document"
    75→    )
    76→    attachments: List[str] = Field(
    77→        default_factory=list, description="List of attachment URLs"
    78→    )
    79→
    80→    # Metadata
    81→    status: RFPStatus = Field(
    82→        default=RFPStatus.DISCOVERED, description="Current processing status"
    83→    )
    84→    naics_codes: List[str] = Field(
    85→        default_factory=list, description="NAICS classification codes"
    86→    )
    87→    set_aside: str = Field(
    88→        default="", description="Set-aside type (e.g., Small Business, 8(a))"
    89→    )
    90→    estimated_value: Optional[float] = Field(
    91→        default=None, description="Estimated contract value in USD"
    92→    )
    93→    contract_type: str = Field(default="", description="Type of contract")
    94→
    95→    # Raw data
    96→    raw_data: dict = Field(
    97→        default_factory=dict, description="Original raw data from scraper"
    98→    )
    99→
   100→    @field_validator("country")
   101→    @classmethod
   102→    def validate_country(cls, v: str) -> str:
   103→        """Validate and normalize country code."""
   104→        if v:
   105→            return v.upper()[:2]
   106→        return "US"
   107→
   108→    def is_us_based(self) -> bool:
   109→        """Check if the RFP is US-based."""
   110→        return self.country.upper() == "US"
   111→
   112→    def age_in_days(self) -> int:
   113→        """Calculate the age of the RFP in days from posted_date."""
   114→        if self.posted_date is None:
   115→            return 0
   116→        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.posted_date
   117→        return delta.days
   118→
   119→    def is_within_age_limit(self, max_age_days: int = 3) -> bool:
   120→        """Check if the RFP is within the age limit."""
   121→        return self.age_in_days() <= max_age_days
   122→
   123→
   124→class ClassificationResult(BaseModel):
   125→    """Model representing the AI classification result for an RFP."""
   126→
   127→    rfp_id: str = Field(..., description="ID of the classified RFP")
   128→    relevance_score: float = Field(
   129→        ..., ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Relevance score between 0 and 1"
   130→    )
   131→    tags: List[RFPTag] = Field(
   132→        default_factory=list, description="Classification tags"
   133→    )
   134→    reasoning: str = Field(
   135→        default="", description="AI reasoning for the classification"
   136→    )
   137→    classified_at: datetime = Field(
   138→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Timestamp of classification"
   139→    )
   140→    model_used: str = Field(
   141→        default="gpt-4o", description="Model used for classification"
   142→    )
   143→    confidence: float = Field(
   144→        default=1.0, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Confidence in the classification"
   145→    )
   146→
   147→    @field_validator("relevance_score", "confidence")
   148→    @classmethod
   149→    def validate_score(cls, v: float) -> float:
   150→        """Ensure score is within valid range."""
   151→        return max(0.0, min(1.0, v))
   152→
   153→    def is_relevant(self, threshold: float = 0.55) -> bool:
   154→        """Check if the RFP meets the relevance threshold."""
   155→        return self.relevance_score >= threshold
   156→
   157→
   158→class ClassifiedRFP(BaseModel):
   159→    """Model combining an RFP with its classification result."""
   160→
   161→    rfp: RFP = Field(..., description="The RFP data")
   162→    classification: ClassificationResult = Field(
   163→        ..., description="Classification result"
   164→    )
   165→
   166→    def is_actionable(self, relevance_threshold: float = 0.55) -> bool:
   167→        """Check if this classified RFP should be actioned (proposal generated)."""
   168→        return (
   169→            self.rfp.is_us_based()
   170→            and self.classification.is_relevant(relevance_threshold)
   171→        )
   172→
   173→
   174→class ProposalMetadata(BaseModel):
   175→    """Model representing metadata for a generated proposal."""
   176→
   177→    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   178→    rfp_id: str = Field(..., description="ID of the associated RFP")
   179→    rfp_title: str = Field(default="", description="Title of the RFP")
   180→    generated_at: datetime = Field(
   181→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="When the proposal was generated"
   182→    )
   183→    version: int = Field(default=1, description="Proposal version number")
   184→
   185→    # Storage locations
   186→    blob_url: str = Field(default="", description="Azure Blob Storage URL")
   187→    blob_path: str = Field(default="", description="Path within the blob container")
   188→
   189→    # Content info
   190→    content_hash: str = Field(
   191→        default="", description="SHA-256 hash of the proposal content"
   192→    )
   193→    word_count: int = Field(default=0, description="Word count of the proposal")
   194→    section_count: int = Field(default=0, description="Number of sections in proposal")
   195→
   196→    # Branding
   197→    brand_name: str = Field(default="NAITIVE", description="Brand name used")
   198→    brand_website: str = Field(
   199→        default="https://www.naitive.cloud", description="Brand website URL"
   200→    )
   201→
   202→    # AI generation info
   203→    model_used: str = Field(
   204→        default="gpt-4o", description="Model used for generation"
   205→    )
   206→    prompt_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Tokens used in prompt")
   207→    completion_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Tokens used in completion")
   208→
   209→
   210→class Proposal(BaseModel):
   211→    """Model representing a complete proposal with content."""
   212→
   213→    metadata: ProposalMetadata = Field(..., description="Proposal metadata")
   214→    markdown_content: str = Field(..., description="Full proposal in markdown format")
   215→
   216→    @property

   218→        """Calculate word count of the proposal content."""
   219→        return len(self.markdown_content.split())

   221→
   222→class DigestEntry(BaseModel):

   242→        return {
   243→            "type": "section",
   244→            "text": {
   245→                "type": "mrkdwn",
   246→                "text": (
   247→                    f"*{self.rfp.title}*\n"

## Summary

**Created:** `src/rfp_radar/digest_builder.py`

**DigestBuilder Class Features:**
- `build_digest()` - Creates Digest models from ClassifiedRFPs and Proposals, sorted by relevance score
- `build_digest_from_results()` - Convenience method for ProposalGenerator.generate_batch() output
- `build_empty_digest()` - Creates empty digest for "no new RFPs" scenarios
- `format_slack_blocks()` - Generates Slack Block Kit formatted messages with:
  - Header with NAITIVE branding
  - Summary statistics (discovered/filtered/relevant/proposals)
  - Entry blocks with score emojis, tags, agency, due dates
  - View RFP buttons and proposal links
  - Truncation notice (max 10 entries)
  - Branded footer
- `format_fallback_text()` - Plain text for notifications
- `format_error_blocks()` / `format_error_fallback()` - Error notification formatting
- `get_score_emoji()` - Score-based emoji selection (⭐/✅/❓)
- Context manager support

   102→    def get_azure_credentials(self):
   103→        """Retrieve Azure credentials, either from environment variables or managed identity."""
   104→        if self._azure_credentials is None:
   105→            self._azure_credentials = self.get_azure_credential(self.AZURE_CLIENT_ID)
   106→        return self._azure_credentials
   107→
   108→    async def get_access_token(self) -> str:
   109→        """Get Azure access token for API calls."""
   110→        try:
   111→            credential = self.get_azure_credentials()
   112→            token = credential.get_token(self.AZURE_COGNITIVE_SERVICES)
   113→            return token.token
   114→        except Exception as e:
   115→            self.logger.error(f"Failed to get access token: {e}")
   116→            raise
   117→
   118→    def _get_required(self, name: str, default: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
   119→        """Get a required configuration value from environment variables.
   120→
   121→        Args:
   122→            name: The name of the environment variable
   123→            default: Optional default value if not found
   124→
   125→        Returns:
   126→            The value of the environment variable or default if provided
   127→
   128→        Raises:
   129→            ValueError: If the environment variable is not found and no default is provided
   130→        """
   131→        if name in os.environ:
   132→            return os.environ[name]
   133→        if default is not None:
   134→            logging.warning(
   135→                "Environment variable %s not found, using default value", name
   136→            )
   137→            return default
   138→        raise ValueError(
   139→            f"Environment variable {name} not found and no default provided"
   140→        )
   141→
   142→    def _get_optional(self, name: str, default: str = "") -> str:
   143→        """Get an optional configuration value from environment variables.
   144→
   145→        Args:
   146→            name: The name of the environment variable
   147→            default: Default value if not found (default: "")
   148→
   149→        Returns:
   150→            The value of the environment variable or the default value
   151→        """
   152→        if name in os.environ:
   153→            return os.environ[name]
   154→        return default
   155→
   156→    def _get_bool(self, name: str) -> bool:
   157→        """Get a boolean configuration value from environment variables.
   158→
   159→        Args:
   160→            name: The name of the environment variable
   161→
   162→        Returns:
   163→            True if the environment variable exists and is set to 'true' or '1', False otherwise
   164→        """
   165→        return name in os.environ and os.environ[name].lower() in ["true", "1"]

1→# slack_client.py
     2→"""Slack SDK wrapper for posting RFP digests to Slack channels."""
     3→
     4→import time
     5→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
     6→
     7→from slack_sdk import WebClient
     8→from slack_sdk.errors import SlackApiError
     9→
    10→from .config import config
    11→from .logging_utils import get_logger
    12→from .models import Digest, DigestEntry
    13→
    14→
    15→class SlackClient:
    16→    """Slack SDK client wrapper for RFP Radar.
    17→
    18→    This class provides methods for posting RFP digests and messages
    19→    to Slack channels. It handles authentication, retries, and error
    20→    handling according to the RFP Radar specifications.
    21→    """
    22→
    23→    # Maximum number of retries for Slack API calls
    24→    MAX_RETRIES = 3
    25→
    26→    # Base delay for exponential backoff (in seconds)
    27→    BASE_RETRY_DELAY = 1.0
    28→
    29→    def __init__(
    30→        self,
    31→        token: Optional[str] = None,
    32→        channel: Optional[str] = None,
    33→    ):
    34→        """Initialize the Slack client.
    35→
    36→        Args:
    37→            token: Slack bot token (xoxb-...). Defaults to config value.
    38→            channel: Default channel to post to. Defaults to config value.
    39→        """
    40→        self.logger = get_logger(__name__)
    41→
    42→        self._token = token or config.SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
    43→        self.default_channel = channel or config.SLACK_CHANNEL
    44→
    45→        # Initialize Slack WebClient
    46→        self._client: Optional[WebClient] = None
    47→
    48→        # Branding configuration
    49→        self.brand_name = config.NAITIVE_BRAND_NAME
    50→        self.brand_website = config.NAITIVE_WEBSITE
    51→
    52→    def _get_client(self) -> WebClient:
    53→        """Get or create the Slack WebClient.
    54→
    55→        Returns:
    56→            Configured WebClient instance
    57→        """
    58→        if self._client is None:
    59→            self._client = WebClient(token=self._token)
    60→        return self._client
    61→
    62→    def _make_request_with_retry(
    63→        self,
    64→        method: str,
    65→        retry_count: int = 0,
    66→        **kwargs: Any,
    67→    ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    68→        """Make a Slack API request with retry logic.
    69→
    70→        Args:
    71→            method: The Slack API method to call
    72→            retry_count: Current retry attempt number
    73→            **kwargs: Arguments to pass to the API method
    74→
    75→        Returns:
    76→            API response dictionary, or None on failure after retries
    77→        """
    78→        client = self._get_client()
    79→
    80→        try:
    81→            # Get the method function from the client
    82→            api_method = getattr(client, method)
    83→            response = api_method(**kwargs)
    84→
    85→            self.logger.debug(
    86→                "Slack API request succeeded",
    87→                extra={
    88→                    "method": method,
    89→                    "channel": kwargs.get("channel"),
    90→                }
    91→            )
    92→
    93→            return response.data
    94→
    95→        except SlackApiError as e:
    96→            error_code = e.response.get("error", "unknown_error")
    97→            self.logger.warning(
    98→                f"Slack API error: {error_code}",
    99→                extra={
   100→                    "method": method,
   101→                    "error": error_code,
   102→                    "retry_count": retry_count,
   103→                }
   104→            )
   105→
   106→            # Retry on rate limiting or transient errors
   107→            retryable_errors = [
   108→                "rate_limited",
   109→                "service_unavailable",
   110→                "internal_error",
   111→                "request_timeout",
   112→            ]
   113→
   114→            if error_code in retryable_errors and retry_count < self.MAX_RETRIES:
   115→                # Get retry delay from headers or use exponential backoff
   116→                retry_after = int(
   117→                    e.response.headers.get("Retry-After", 2 ** retry_count)
   118→                )
   119→                delay = max(retry_after, self.BASE_RETRY_DELAY * (2 ** retry_count))
   120→
   121→                self.logger.info(
   122→                    f"Retrying Slack API request after {delay}s",
   123→                    extra={"retry_count": retry_count + 1}
   124→                )
   125→                time.sleep(delay)
   126→
   127→                return self._make_request_with_retry(
   128→                    method=method,
   129→                    retry_count=retry_count + 1,
   130→                    **kwargs,
   131→                )
   132→
   133→            # Log error but don't fail - per spec, don't block on Slack failures
   134→            self.logger.error(
   135→                f"Slack API request failed after {retry_count} retries: {e}",
   136→                extra={
   137→                    "method": method,
   138→                    "error": error_code,
   139→                }
   140→            )
   141→            return None
   142→
   143→        except Exception as e:
   144→            self.logger.error(
   145→                f"Unexpected error in Slack API request: {e}",
   146→                extra={"method": method}
   147→            )
   148→            return None
   149→
   150→    def post_message(
   151→        self,
   152→        text: str,
   153→        channel: Optional[str] = None,
   154→        blocks: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,
   155→        thread_ts: Optional[str] = None,
   156→        unfurl_links: bool = False,
   157→    ) -> Optional[str]:
   158→        """Post a message to a Slack channel.
   159→
   160→        Args:
   161→            text: The message text (used as fallback for notifications)
   162→            channel: Channel to post to (defaults to configured channel)
   163→            blocks: Optional Slack blocks for rich formatting
   164→            thread_ts: Optional thread timestamp to reply to
   165→            unfurl_links: Whether to unfurl URLs in the message
   166→
   167→        Returns:
   168→            Message timestamp (ts) if successful, None on failure
   169→        """
   170→        target_channel = channel or self.default_channel
   171→
   172→        self.logger.info(
   173→            "Posting message to Slack",
   174→            extra={
   175→                "channel": target_channel,
   176→                "has_blocks": blocks is not None,
   177→            }
   178→        )
   179→
   180→        response = self._make_request_with_retry(
   181→            "chat_postMessage",
   182→            channel=target_channel,
   183→            text=text,
   184→            blocks=blocks,
   185→            thread_ts=thread_ts,
   186→            unfurl_links=unfurl_links,
   187→        )
   188→
   189→        if response and response.get("ok"):
   190→            message_ts = response.get("ts")
   191→            self.logger.info(
   192→                "Message posted successfully",
   193→                extra={
   194→                    "channel": target_channel,
   195→                    "message_ts": message_ts,
   196→                }
   197→            )
   198→            return message_ts
   199→
   200→        return None
   201→
   202→    def post_digest(
   203→        self,
   204→        digest: Digest,
   205→        channel: Optional[str] = None,
   206→    ) -> Optional[str]:
   207→        """Post an RFP digest to a Slack channel.
   208→
   209→        Args:
   210→            digest: The Digest model containing RFP entries
   211→            channel: Channel to post to (defaults to configured channel)
   212→
   213→        Returns:
   214→            Message timestamp (ts) if successful, None on failure
   215→        """
   216→        target_channel = channel or self.default_channel
   217→
   218→        # Build the digest message
   219→        blocks = self._build_digest_blocks(digest)
   220→        fallback_text = self._build_digest_fallback(digest)
   221→
   222→        self.logger.info(
   223→            "Posting RFP digest to Slack",
   224→            extra={
   225→                "channel": target_channel,
   226→                "entry_count": len(digest.entries),
   227→                "total_discovered": digest.total_discovered,
   228→                "total_relevant": digest.total_relevant,
   229→            }
   230→        )
   231→
   232→        return self.post_message(
   233→            text=fallback_text,
   234→            channel=target_channel,
   235→            blocks=blocks,
   236→        )
   237→
   238→    def _build_digest_blocks(self, digest: Digest) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   239→        """Build Slack blocks for a digest message.
   240→
   241→        Args:
   242→            digest: The Digest model
   243→
   244→        Returns:
   245→            List of Slack block dictionaries
   246→        """
   247→        blocks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   248→
   249→        # Header block
   250→        blocks.append({
   251→            "type": "header",
   252→            "text": {
   253→                "type": "plain_text",
   254→                "text": f":radar: {self.brand_name} RFP Radar Daily Digest",
   255→                "emoji": True,
   256→            },
   257→        })
   258→
   259→        # Summary section
   260→        if digest.is_empty():
   261→            blocks.append({
   262→                "type": "section",
   263→                "text": {
   264→                    "type": "mrkdwn",
   265→                    "text": (
   266→                        ":information_source: *No new relevant RFPs today.*\n\n"
   267→                        f"• Discovered: {digest.total_discovered} RFPs\n"
   268→                        f"• After filtering: {digest.total_filtered} RFPs\n"

1→"""Base scraper abstract class for RFP Radar.
     2→
     3→This module provides the abstract base class that all RFP scrapers must inherit from.
     4→It defines the common interface and provides shared functionality for:
     5→- HTTP requests with retry logic and exponential backoff
     6→- RFP filtering by age and geography
     7→- Structured logging
     8→- Error handling
     9→"""
    10→
    11→import logging
    12→import time
    13→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
    14→from datetime import datetime
    15→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    16→
    17→import requests
    18→from requests.exceptions import RequestException
    19→
    20→from rfp_radar.models import RFP, RFPSource, ScraperResult
    21→
    22→
    23→class BaseScraper(ABC):
    24→    """Abstract base class for RFP scrapers.
    25→
    26→    All concrete scraper implementations must inherit from this class and
    27→    implement the required abstract methods. The base class provides:
    28→    - HTTP request handling with retry logic
    29→    - Common filtering by age and geography
    30→    - Structured logging
    31→    - Error handling patterns
    32→
    33→    Attributes:
    34→        source: The RFPSource enum value for this scraper
    35→        base_url: Base URL for the scraper's target portal

    54→    """
    55→
    56→    # Class-level attributes to be overridden by subclasses
    57→    source: RFPSource = RFPSource.MANUAL
    58→    base_url: str = ""
    59→

    64→
    65→    def __init__(
    66→        self,
    67→        max_age_days: int = 3,
    68→        us_only: bool = True,
    69→        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
    70→    ):
    71→        """Initialize the base scraper.
    72→
    73→        Args:
    74→            max_age_days: Maximum age in days for RFPs to include (default: 3)
    75→            us_only: If True, filter to US-based RFPs only (default: True)
    76→            logger: Optional logger instance. If None, creates one.
    77→        """
    78→        self.max_age_days = max_age_days
    79→        self.us_only = us_only
    80→        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
    81→        self.session = requests.Session()
    82→        self._configure_session()
    83→
    84→    def _configure_session(self) -> None:
    85→        """Configure the requests session with default headers."""
    86→        self.session.headers.update({
    87→            "User-Agent": "NAITIVE-RFP-Radar/1.0",
    88→            "Accept": "application/json",
    89→            "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    90→        })
    91→
    92→    def close(self) -> None:
    93→        """Close the requests session and release resources."""
    94→        if self.session:
    95→            self.session.close()
    96→
    97→    def __enter__(self):
    98→        """Context manager entry."""
    99→        return self
   100→
   101→    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
   102→        """Context manager exit - closes session."""
   103→        self.close()
   104→        return False
   105→
   106→    @abstractmethod
   107→    def fetch_listings(self, **kwargs) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   108→        """Fetch raw RFP listings from the source portal.
   109→
   110→        This method must be implemented by all concrete scraper classes.
   111→        It should return a list of raw data dictionaries that can be
   112→        parsed into RFP objects.
   113→
   114→        Args:

   116→

   119→

   121→            RequestException: If the request fails after all retries
   122→        """
   123→        pass
   124→
   125→    @abstractmethod
   126→    def parse_listing(self, raw_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[RFP]:
   127→        """Parse a raw listing dictionary into an RFP object.
   128→
   129→        This method must be implemented by all concrete scraper classes.
   130→        It should handle the source-specific data format and create
   131→        a normalized RFP object.
   132→
   133→        Args:
   134→            raw_data: Raw listing data from the source
   135→
   136→        Returns:
   137→            RFP object if parsing succeeds, None if the data is invalid
   138→        """
   139→        pass
   140→
   141→    def scrape(self, **kwargs) -> ScraperResult:
   142→        """Execute the full scraping pipeline.
   143→
   144→        This method orchestrates the complete scraping process:
   145→        1. Fetch raw listings from the source
   146→        2. Parse each listing into RFP objects
   147→        3. Filter by age and geography
   148→        4. Return the results
   149→
   150→        Args:
   151→            **kwargs: Source-specific parameters passed to fetch_listings
   152→
   153→        Returns:
   154→            ScraperResult containing the scraped and filtered RFPs
   155→        """
   156→        start_time = time.time()
   157→        self.logger.info(
   158→            "Starting scrape",
   159→            extra={
   160→                "source": self.source.value,
   161→                "max_age_days": self.max_age_days,
   162→                "us_only": self.us_only,
   163→            },
   164→        )
   165→
   166→        try:
   167→            # Fetch raw listings
   168→            raw_listings = self.fetch_listings(**kwargs)
   169→            total_found = len(raw_listings)
   170→            self.logger.info(
   171→                "Fetched listings",
   172→                extra={"source": self.source.value, "count": total_found},
   173→            )
   174→
   175→            # Parse and filter
   176→            rfps = []
   177→            for raw_data in raw_listings:
   178→                try:
   179→                    rfp = self.parse_listing(raw_data)
   180→                    if rfp is not None:

   182→                        if self._passes_filters(rfp):
   183→                            rfps.append(rfp)
   184→                except Exception as e:

   191→                        },

   203→                },

   213→            )

entries.content: Now let me update the subtask status to completed:

## Summary

I've successfully completed **subtask-5-1**: Create main.py orchestrator with full pipeline logic.

### What was created

**File:** `src/rfp_radar/main.py`

The main orchestrator module implements the complete RFP Radar pipeline with:

1. **RFPRadarPipeline class** - Main orchestrator with:
   - Context manager support (`with` statement)
   - Lazy initialization of all client dependencies
   - Comprehensive pipeline statistics tracking
   - Health check for all components

2. **Pipeline stages:**
   - `_scrape_all_sources()` - Scrapes from all registered portal scrapers
   - `_classify_rfps()` - AI classification using Azure OpenAI
   - `_filter_relevant()` - Filters by relevance threshold (default 0.55)
   - `_ensure_azure_resources()` - Creates container/index if needed
   - `_store_rfp_metadata()` - Stores in Azure Blob Storage
   - `_index_rfps()` - Indexes in Azure AI Search
   - `_generate_proposals()` - Generates Level 3 proposals
   - `_build_and_send_digest()` - Sends digest to Slack

3. **main() function** - Entry point that:
   - Sets up structured logging
   - Creates and runs the pipeline
   - Returns exit code (0 success, 1 failure)

### Key features
- Graceful Slack failure handling (logs but doesn't block)
- Error notifications sent to Slack on pipeline failure
- Full integration with all existing modules

### Verification
✅ Import verification passed using `.venv/bin/python3`

1→# main.py
     2→"""RFP Radar Main Orchestrator.
     3→
     4→This module provides the main entry point for the RFP Radar service.
     5→It orchestrates the complete pipeline:
     6→    1. Scrape RFPs from multiple portals
     7→    2. Filter by age and geography (done in scrapers)
     8→    3. Classify RFPs using Azure OpenAI
     9→    4. Filter by relevance threshold
    10→    5. Store RFPs in Azure Blob Storage
    11→    6. Index RFPs in Azure AI Search
    12→    7. Generate proposals for relevant RFPs
    13→    8. Build digest and post to Slack
    14→"""
    15→
    16→import sys
    17→import time
    18→from datetime import datetime
    19→from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
    20→
    21→from .classifier import RFPClassifier
    22→from .config import config
    23→from .digest_builder import DigestBuilder
    24→from .logging_utils import get_logger, setup_logging
    25→from .models import (
    26→    ClassifiedRFP,
    27→    Digest,
    28→    Proposal,
    29→    RFP,
    30→    RFPStatus,
    31→    ScraperResult,
    32→)
    33→from .proposal_generator import ProposalGenerator
    34→from .scrapers import SCRAPERS, get_available_sources
    35→from .search_client import SearchClient
    36→from .slack_client import SlackClient
    37→from .storage_client import StorageClient
    38→
    39→
    40→class RFPRadarPipeline:
    41→    """Main orchestrator for the RFP Radar processing pipeline.
    42→
    43→    This class coordinates all components of the RFP Radar system:
    44→    - Scrapers for fetching RFPs from government portals
    45→    - Classifier for AI-based relevance scoring
    46→    - Storage and Search clients for Azure integration
    47→    - Proposal generator for creating proposals
    48→    - Slack client for notifications
    49→
    50→    Attributes:
    51→        classifier: RFPClassifier instance for AI classification.
    52→        proposal_generator: ProposalGenerator instance for proposal creation.
    53→        storage_client: StorageClient for Azure Blob Storage.
    54→        search_client: SearchClient for Azure AI Search.
    55→        slack_client: SlackClient for Slack notifications.
    56→        digest_builder: DigestBuilder for formatting digests.
    57→    """
    58→
    59→    def __init__(
    60→        self,
    61→        classifier: Optional[RFPClassifier] = None,
    62→        proposal_generator: Optional[ProposalGenerator] = None,
    63→        storage_client: Optional[StorageClient] = None,
    64→        search_client: Optional[SearchClient] = None,
    65→        slack_client: Optional[SlackClient] = None,
    66→        digest_builder: Optional[DigestBuilder] = None,
    67→    ):
    68→        """Initialize the RFP Radar pipeline.
    69→
    70→        Args:
    71→            classifier: Optional RFPClassifier instance.
    72→            proposal_generator: Optional ProposalGenerator instance.
    73→            storage_client: Optional StorageClient instance.
    74→            search_client: Optional SearchClient instance.
    75→            slack_client: Optional SlackClient instance.
    76→            digest_builder: Optional DigestBuilder instance.
    77→        """
    78→        self.logger = get_logger(__name__)
    79→
    80→        # Initialize clients (lazy initialization for optional overrides)
    81→        self._classifier = classifier
    82→        self._proposal_generator = proposal_generator
    83→        self._storage_client = storage_client
    84→        self._search_client = search_client
    85→        self._slack_client = slack_client
    86→        self._digest_builder = digest_builder
    87→
    88→        # Track ownership for cleanup
    89→        self._owns_classifier = classifier is None
    90→        self._owns_proposal_generator = proposal_generator is None
    91→        self._owns_storage_client = storage_client is None
    92→        self._owns_search_client = search_client is None
    93→        self._owns_slack_client = slack_client is None
    94→
    95→        # Pipeline statistics
    96→        self.stats = {
    97→            "start_time": None,
    98→            "end_time": None,
    99→            "total_discovered": 0,
   100→            "total_filtered": 0,
   101→            "total_classified": 0,
   102→            "total_relevant": 0,
   103→            "total_proposals": 0,
   104→            "scraper_results": {},
   105→            "errors": [],
   106→        }
   107→
   108→    @property
   109→    def classifier(self) -> RFPClassifier:
   110→        """Get or create the RFP classifier."""
   111→        if self._classifier is None:
   112→            self._classifier = RFPClassifier()
   113→        return self._classifier
   114→
   115→    @property
   116→    def proposal_generator(self) -> ProposalGenerator:
   117→        """Get or create the proposal generator."""
   118→        if self._proposal_generator is None:
   119→            self._proposal_generator = ProposalGenerator()
   120→        return self._proposal_generator
   121→
   122→    @property
   123→    def storage_client(self) -> StorageClient:
   124→        """Get or create the storage client."""
   125→        if self._storage_client is None:
   126→            self._storage_client = StorageClient()
   127→        return self._storage_client
   128→
   129→    @property
   130→    def search_client(self) -> SearchClient:
   131→        """Get or create the search client."""
   132→        if self._search_client is None:
   133→            self._search_client = SearchClient()
   134→        return self._search_client
   135→
   136→    @property
   137→    def slack_client(self) -> SlackClient:
   138→        """Get or create the Slack client."""
   139→        if self._slack_client is None:
   140→            self._slack_client = SlackClient()
   141→        return self._slack_client
   142→
   143→    @property
   144→    def digest_builder(self) -> DigestBuilder:
   145→        """Get or create the digest builder."""
   146→        if self._digest_builder is None:
   147→            self._digest_builder = DigestBuilder()
   148→        return self._digest_builder
   149→
   150→    def run(self) -> Digest:
   151→        """Execute the complete RFP Radar pipeline.
   152→
   153→        This method orchestrates the full processing flow:
   154→        1. Scrape RFPs from all available sources
   155→        2. Classify RFPs using AI
   156→        3. Filter by relevance threshold
   157→        4. Store RFPs and metadata
   158→        5. Index in Azure AI Search
   159→        6. Generate proposals
   160→        7. Build and send digest
   161→
   162→        Returns:
   163→            Digest object containing the pipeline results.
   164→        """
   165→        self.stats["start_time"] = datetime.utcnow()
   166→        self.logger.info("Starting RFP Radar pipeline")
   167→
   168→        try:
   169→            # Step 1: Scrape RFPs from all sources
   170→            all_rfps = self._scrape_all_sources()
   171→
   172→            # Step 2: Classify RFPs
   173→            classified_rfps = self._classify_rfps(all_rfps)
   174→
   175→            # Step 3: Filter by relevance threshold
   176→            relevant_rfps = self._filter_relevant(classified_rfps)
   177→
   178→            # Step 4: Ensure Azure resources exist
   179→            self._ensure_azure_resources()
   180→
   181→            # Step 5: Store RFP metadata
   182→            self._store_rfp_metadata(relevant_rfps)
   183→
   184→            # Step 6: Index RFPs in Azure AI Search
   185→            self._index_rfps(relevant_rfps)
   186→
   187→            # Step 7: Generate proposals for relevant RFPs
   188→            proposals = self._generate_proposals(relevant_rfps)
   189→
   190→            # Step 8: Update search index with proposal URLs
   191→            self._update_search_with_proposals(proposals)
   192→
   193→            # Step 9: Build and send digest
   194→            digest = self._build_and_send_digest(relevant_rfps, proposals)
   195→
   196→            self.stats["end_time"] = datetime.utcnow()
   197→            self._log_summary()
   198→
   199→            return digest
   200→
   201→        except Exception as e:
   202→            self.logger.error(
   203→                f"Pipeline failed with error: {e}",
   204→                extra={"error": str(e)}
   205→            )
   206→            self.stats["errors"].append(str(e))
   207→            self.stats["end_time"] = datetime.utcnow()
   208→
   209→            # Attempt to send error notification
   210→            self._send_error_notification(str(e))
   211→
   212→            raise
   213→
   214→    def _scrape_all_sources(self) -> List[RFP]:
   215→        """Scrape RFPs from all available sources.
   216→
   217→        Returns:
   218→            List of RFP objects from all scrapers.
   219→        """
   220→        self.logger.info("Starting scraping from all sources")
   221→
   222→        all_rfps: List[RFP] = []
   223→        available_sources = get_available_sources()
   224→
   225→        if not available_sources:
   226→            self.logger.warning("No scrapers registered")
   227→            return all_rfps
   228→
   229→        for source_name in available_sources:
   230→            try:
   231→                scraper_class = SCRAPERS.get(source_name)
   232→                if scraper_class is None:
   233→                    continue
   234→
   235→                self.logger.info(
   236→                    f"Scraping source: {source_name}",
   237→                    extra={"source": source_name}
   238→                )
   239→
   240→                with scraper_class(
   241→                    max_age_days=config.RFP_MAX_AGE_DAYS,
   242→                    us_only=True,
   243→                ) as scraper:
   244→                    result: ScraperResult = scraper.scrape()
   245→
   246→                    self.stats["scraper_results"][source_name] = {
   247→                        "success": result.success,
   248→                        "total_found": result.total_found,
   249→

## Summary

Successfully completed **subtask-6-3**: Create Dockerfile with multi-stage build using uv

### What was done:
1. **Read pattern file** (`src/mcp_server/Dockerfile`) to understand the multi-stage build approach
2. **Analyzed rfp_radar service** - determined it's a batch processing pipeline (not a web server), so no port exposure or health check needed
3. **Created `src/rfp_radar/Dockerfile`** following the pattern:
   - Multi-stage build with `base` and `builder` stages
   - Uses `mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.11-bullseye` base image
   - Copies `uv` binaries from `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.6.3`
   - Installs dependencies with `uv sync --frozen --no-dev`
   - Creates non-root `app` user for security
   - CMD runs `python -m rfp_radar.main` for batch execution

4. **Verification passed**: `test -f src/rfp_radar/Dockerfile && echo 'OK'` → OK
5. **Committed changes** with appropriate message
6. **Updated implementation plan** - marked subtask as completed

entries.detail:
1→# Specification: NAITIVE RFP Radar Deployment
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Deploy the NAITIVE RFP Radar system - an Azure-native automated RFP (Request for Proposal) discovery and proposal generation service. This system scrapes government RFP portals, filters opportunities by age and geography (US-only), uses Azure OpenAI to score relevance and extract tags, stores metadata and proposals in Azure Blob Storage and AI Search, generates Level 3 full proposals in markdown format, and delivers daily digests to a Slack channel (#bots). The goal is to create an "RFP scouting + proposal-writing team that never sleeps."
     6→
     7→## Workflow Type
     8→
     9→**Type**: feature
    10→
    11→**Rationale**: This is a new feature deployment that adds a complete RFP Radar subsystem to the existing Multi-Agent Custom Automation Engine. It involves creating new Python modules, a new Docker container, infrastructure provisioning via Bicep, and integration with external services (Slack, government portals). The scope is significant but well-defined with clear deliverables.
    12→
    13→## Task Scope
    14→
    15→### Services Involved
    16→- **rfp-radar** (primary) - New Python service for RFP scraping, classification, proposal generation, and Slack delivery
    17→- **azure-infrastructure** (integration) - Bicep templates to provision Azure Container Apps Job, Blob Storage container, Search index
    18→- **slack-integration** (integration) - Webhook/bot integration for delivering RFP digests
    19→
    20→### This Task Will:
    21→- [ ] Create new `src/rfp_radar/` Python module with orchestrator, scrapers, classifier, proposal generator, and digest builder
    22→- [ ] Create Dockerfile for RFP Radar containerization
    23→- [ ] Add Bicep modules for RFP Radar Azure infrastructure (Container Apps Job, dedicated blob container)
    24→- [ ] Configure environment variables and secrets for RFP Radar
    25→- [ ] Implement 3 scraper templates (GovTribe, OpenGov, BidNet patterns)
    26→- [ ] Integrate with existing Azure OpenAI deployment for classification
    27→- [ ] Configure Slack bot delivery to #bots channel
    28→- [ ] Add deployment script for RFP Radar service
    29→

   125→
   126→### Environment Configuration Pattern
   127→
   128→From `src/backend/common/config/app_config.py`:
   129→
   130→```python
   131→class AppConfig:
   132→    def __init__(self):
   133→        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   134→        # Required vs optional pattern
   135→        self.REQUIRED_VAR = self._get_required("VAR_NAME")
   136→        self.OPTIONAL_VAR = self._get_optional("VAR_NAME", "default")
   137→
   138→    def _get_required(self, name: str, default: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
   139→        if name in os.environ:
   140→            return os.environ[name]
   141→        if default is not None:
   142→            return default
   143→        raise ValueError(f"Environment variable {name} not found")
   144→
   145→    def _get_optional(self, name: str, default: str = "") -> str:
   146→        return os.environ.get(name, default)
   147→```
   148→
   149→**Key Points:**
   150→- Use `_get_required()` for mandatory configuration
   151→- Use `_get_optional()` for optional configuration with defaults
   152→- Load via `python-dotenv` for local development

   136→            )
   137→            return default
   138→        raise ValueError(
   139→            f"Environment variable {name} not found and no default provided"
   140→        )
   141→
   142→    def _get_optional(self, name: str, default: str = "") -> str:
   143→        """Get an optional configuration value from environment variables.
   144→
   145→        Args:
   146→            name: The name of the environment variable
   147→            default: Default value if not found (default: "")
   148→
   149→        Returns:
   150→            The value of the environment variable or the default value
   151→        """
   152→        if name in os.environ:
   153→            return os.environ[name]
   154→        return default
   155→
   156→    def _get_bool(self, name: str) -> bool:
   157→        """Get a boolean configuration value from environment variables.
   158→
   159→        Args:
   160→            name: The name of the environment variable
   161→
   162→        Returns:
   163→            True if the environment variable exists and is set to 'true' or '1', False otherwise
   164→        """
   165→        return name in os.environ and os.environ[name].lower() in ["true", "1"]
   166→
   167→
   168→# Create a global instance of RFPRadarConfig
   169→config = RFPRadarConfig()
   170→

## Completed: Create Bicep module for Container Apps Job

### Created File
- `infra/modules/rfp-radar-job.bicep`

### Features Implemented
The module creates an Azure Container Apps Job (`Microsoft.App/jobs`) with:

1. **Scheduled Trigger**: Cron-based schedule (default: `0 6 * * *` - daily at 6 AM UTC)
2. **Managed Identity**: User-assigned identity for secure Azure resource access
3. **Environment Variables**:
   - Azure Storage (blob URL, container name)
   - Azure AI Search (endpoint, index name)
   - Azure OpenAI (endpoint, deployment, API version)
   - Slack (channel, bot token via Key Vault secret)
   - RFP Radar configuration (relevance threshold, max age days)
   - NAITIVE branding (brand name, website)
   - Application Insights connection string

4. **Job Configuration**:
   - Configurable parallelism (default: 1)
   - Replica completion count (default: 1)
   - Retry limit (default: 3)
   - Timeout (default: 30 minutes)

5. **Security**: Slack Bot Token stored as Key Vault secret reference

6. **Outputs**: Resource name, ID, location, and resource group

### Verification
- ✅ File exists: `test -f infra/modules/rfp-radar-job.bicep` returns 'OK'

## ✅ Subtask 8-1 Complete

**Created:** `.github/workflows/rfp-radar-deploy.yml`

### Summary

Created the GitHub Actions workflow for building and deploying the RFP Radar Docker image, following the patterns from `docker-build-and-push.yml`:

**Key Features:**
- **Triggers:** Push and PR to `main`, `dev-v3`, `demo-v3`, `hotfix` branches
- **Path filtering:** Only triggers on changes to `src/rfp_radar/**` or the workflow file itself
- **Docker Build:** Uses `docker/build-push-action@v6` with buildx
- **ACR Push:** Pushes to Azure Container Registry on protected branches
- **Tagging Strategy:**
  - Branch-based tags: `latest_v3`, `dev_v3`, `demo_v3`, `hotfix`
  - Historical tags: `{tag}_{date}_{run_number}` for version tracking
- **Image name:** `macaerfpradar` (follows `macaebackend`, `macaefrontend`, `macaemcp` pattern)

1→"""Pydantic models for RFP Radar data structures."""
     2→
     3→import uuid
     4→from datetime import datetime
     5→from enum import Enum
     6→from typing import List, Optional
     7→
     8→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
     9→
    10→
    11→class RFPSource(str, Enum):
    12→    """Enumeration of RFP data sources."""
    13→
    14→    GOVTRIBE = "govtribe"
    15→    OPENGOV = "opengov"
    16→    BIDNET = "bidnet"
    17→    MANUAL = "manual"
    18→
    19→
    20→class RFPStatus(str, Enum):
    21→    """Enumeration of RFP processing status."""
    22→
    23→    DISCOVERED = "discovered"
    24→    FILTERED = "filtered"
    25→    CLASSIFIED = "classified"
    26→    STORED = "stored"
    27→    PROPOSAL_GENERATED = "proposal_generated"
    28→    NOTIFIED = "notified"
    29→    SKIPPED = "skipped"
    30→    ERROR = "error"
    31→
    32→
    33→class RFPTag(str, Enum):
    34→    """Enumeration of RFP classification tags."""
    35→
    36→    AI = "AI"
    37→    DYNAMICS = "Dynamics"
    38→    MODERNIZATION = "Modernization"
    39→    CLOUD = "Cloud"
    40→    SECURITY = "Security"
    41→    DATA = "Data"
    42→    AUTOMATION = "Automation"
    43→    OTHER = "Other"
    44→
    45→
    46→class RFP(BaseModel):
    47→    """Model representing a Request for Proposal."""
    48→
    49→    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    50→    title: str = Field(..., description="RFP title")
    51→    description: str = Field(default="", description="RFP description or summary")
    52→    agency: str = Field(default="", description="Issuing agency or organization")
    53→    source: RFPSource = Field(default=RFPSource.MANUAL, description="Source portal")
    54→    source_url: str = Field(default="", description="Original URL of the RFP listing")
    55→
    56→    # Dates
    57→    posted_date: Optional[datetime] = Field(
    58→        default=None, description="Date the RFP was posted"
    59→    )
    60→    due_date: Optional[datetime] = Field(
    61→        default=None, description="Proposal submission deadline"
    62→    )
    63→    discovered_at: datetime = Field(
    64→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="When we discovered this RFP"
    65→    )
    66→
    67→    # Geography
    68→    location: str = Field(default="", description="Geographic location or region")
    69→    country: str = Field(default="US", description="Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)")
    70→    state: str = Field(default="", description="State or region code")
    71→
    72→    # Documents
    73→    pdf_url: Optional[str] = Field(
    74→        default=None, description="URL to the primary RFP document"
    75→    )
    76→    attachments: List[str] = Field(
    77→        default_factory=list, description="List of attachment URLs"
    78→    )
    79→
    80→    # Metadata
    81→    status: RFPStatus = Field(
    82→        default=RFPStatus.DISCOVERED, description="Current processing status"
    83→    )
    84→    naics_codes: List[str] = Field(
    85→        default_factory=list, description="NAICS classification codes"
    86→    )
    87→    set_aside: str = Field(
    88→        default="", description="Set-aside type (e.g., Small Business, 8(a))"
    89→    )
    90→    estimated_value: Optional[float] = Field(
    91→        default=None, description="Estimated contract value in USD"
    92→    )
    93→    contract_type: str = Field(default="", description="Type of contract")
    94→
    95→    # Raw data
    96→    raw_data: dict = Field(
    97→        default_factory=dict, description="Original raw data from scraper"
    98→    )
    99→
   100→    @field_validator("country")
   101→    @classmethod
   102→    def validate_country(cls, v: str) -> str:
   103→        """Validate and normalize country code."""
   104→        if v:
   105→            return v.upper()[:2]
   106→        return "US"
   107→
   108→    def is_us_based(self) -> bool:
   109→        """Check if the RFP is US-based."""
   110→        return self.country.upper() == "US"
   111→
   112→    def age_in_days(self) -> int:
   113→        """Calculate the age of the RFP in days from posted_date."""
   114→        if self.posted_date is None:
   115→            return 0
   116→        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.posted_date
   117→        return delta.days
   118→
   119→    def is_within_age_limit(self, max_age_days: int = 3) -> bool:
   120→        """Check if the RFP is within the age limit."""
   121→        return self.age_in_days() <= max_age_days
   122→
   123→
   124→class ClassificationResult(BaseModel):
   125→    """Model representing the AI classification result for an RFP."""
   126→
   127→    rfp_id: str = Field(..., description="ID of the classified RFP")
   128→    relevance_score: float = Field(
   129→        ..., ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Relevance score between 0 and 1"
   130→    )
   131→    tags: List[RFPTag] = Field(
   132→        default_factory=list, description="Classification tags"
   133→    )
   134→    reasoning: str = Field(
   135→        default="", description="AI reasoning for the classification"
   136→    )
   137→    classified_at: datetime = Field(
   138→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Timestamp of classification"
   139→    )
   140→    model_used: str = Field(
   141→        default="gpt-4o", description="Model used for classification"
   142→    )
   143→    confidence: float = Field(
   144→        default=1.0, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Confidence in the classification"
   145→    )
   146→
   147→    @field_validator("relevance_score", "confidence")
   148→    @classmethod
   149→    def validate_score(cls, v: float) -> float:
   150→        """Ensure score is within valid range."""
   151→        return max(0.0, min(1.0, v))
   152→
   153→    def is_relevant(self, threshold: float = 0.55) -> bool:
   154→        """Check if the RFP meets the relevance threshold."""
   155→        return self.relevance_score >= threshold
   156→
   157→
   158→class ClassifiedRFP(BaseModel):
   159→    """Model combining an RFP with its classification result."""
   160→
   161→    rfp: RFP = Field(..., description="The RFP data")
   162→    classification: ClassificationResult = Field(
   163→        ..., description="Classification result"
   164→    )
   165→
   166→    def is_actionable(self, relevance_threshold: float = 0.55) -> bool:
   167→        """Check if this classified RFP should be actioned (proposal generated)."""
   168→        return (
   169→            self.rfp.is_us_based()
   170→            and self.classification.is_relevant(relevance_threshold)
   171→        )
   172→
   173→
   174→class ProposalMetadata(BaseModel):
   175→    """Model representing metadata for a generated proposal."""
   176→
   177→    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   178→    rfp_id: str = Field(..., description="ID of the associated RFP")
   179→    rfp_title: str = Field(default="", description="Title of the RFP")
   180→    generated_at: datetime = Field(
   181→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="When the proposal was generated"
   182→    )
   183→    version: int = Field(default=1, description="Proposal version number")
   184→
   185→    # Storage locations
   186→    blob_url: str = Field(default="", description="Azure Blob Storage URL")
   187→    blob_path: str = Field(default="", description="Path within the blob container")
   188→
   189→    # Content info
   190→    content_hash: str = Field(
   191→        default="", description="SHA-256 hash of the proposal content"
   192→    )
   193→    word_count: int = Field(default=0, description="Word count of the proposal")
   194→    section_count: int = Field(default=0, description="Number of sections in proposal")
   195→
   196→    # Branding
   197→    brand_name: str = Field(default="NAITIVE", description="Brand name used")
   198→    brand_website: str = Field(
   199→        default="https://www.naitive.cloud", description="Brand website URL"
   200→    )
   201→
   202→    # AI generation info
   203→    model_used: str = Field(
   204→        default="gpt-4o", description="Model used for generation"
   205→    )
   206→    prompt_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Tokens used in prompt")
   207→    completion_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Tokens used in completion")
   208→
   209→
   210→class Proposal(BaseModel):
   211→    """Model representing a complete proposal with content."""
   212→
   213→    metadata: ProposalMetadata = Field(..., description="Proposal metadata")
   214→    markdown_content: str = Field(..., description="Full proposal in markdown format")
   215→
   216→    @property
   217→    def word_count(self) -> int:
   218→        """Calculate word count of the proposal content."""
   219→        return len(self.markdown_content.split())
   220→
   221→
   222→class DigestEntry(BaseModel):
   223→    """Model representing a single entry in the daily digest."""
   224→
   225→    rfp: RFP = Field(..., description="The RFP data")
   226→    classification: ClassificationResult = Field(
   227→        ..., description="Classification result"
   228→    )
   229→    proposal_url: Optional[str] = Field(
   230→        default=None, description="URL to the generated proposal"
   231→    )
   232→
   233→    def to_slack_block(self) -> dict:
   234→        """Convert to a Slack block format."""
   235→        tags_str = ", ".join([tag.value for tag in self.classification.tags])
   236→        score_emoji = (
   237→            ":star:" if self.classification.relevance_score >= 0.8
   238→            else ":white_check_mark:" if self.classification.relevance_score >= 0.55
   239→            else ":grey_question:"
   240→        )
   241→
   242→        return {
   243→            "type": "section",
   244→            "text": {
   245→                "type": "mrkdwn",
   246→                "text": (
   247→                    f"*{self.rfp.title}*\n"
   248→                    f"{score_emoji} Score: {self.classification.relevance_score:.2f} | "
   249→                    f"

1→# classifier.py
     2→"""RFP Classifier module for AI relevance scoring and tag extraction.
     3→
     4→This module provides the RFPClassifier class which uses Azure OpenAI
     5→to classify RFPs by relevance to NAITIVE's capabilities and extract
     6→classification tags.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→from datetime import datetime
    10→from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
    11→
    12→from .config import config
    13→from .llm_client import LLMClient
    14→from .logging_utils import get_logger
    15→from .models import (
    16→    ClassificationResult,
    17→    ClassifiedRFP,
    18→    RFP,
    19→    RFPStatus,
    20→    RFPTag,
    21→)
    22→
    23→
    24→class RFPClassifier:
    25→    """Classifier for RFP relevance scoring and tag extraction.
    26→
    27→    This class wraps the LLMClient to provide high-level RFP classification
    28→    functionality, including batch processing, filtering by relevance
    29→    threshold, and proper model conversion.
    30→
    31→    Attributes:
    32→        relevance_threshold: Minimum relevance score for an RFP to be
    33→            considered relevant (default: 0.55).
    34→        llm_client: The LLMClient instance used for AI classification.
    35→    """
    36→
    37→    # Valid tags that can be extracted from classification
    38→    VALID_TAGS = {tag.value for tag in RFPTag}
    39→
    40→    def __init__(
    41→        self,
    42→        llm_client: Optional[LLMClient] = None,
    43→        relevance_threshold: Optional[float] = None,
    44→    ):
    45→        """Initialize the RFP Classifier.
    46→
    47→        Args:
    48→            llm_client: Optional LLMClient instance. If not provided,
    49→                       a new client will be created using config settings.
    50→            relevance_threshold: Optional relevance threshold override.
    51→                               Defaults to config.RFP_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD.
    52→        """
    53→        self.logger = get_logger(__name__)
    54→
    55→        # Use provided client or create a new one
    56→        self._llm_client = llm_client
    57→        self._owns_client = llm_client is None
    58→
    59→        # Set relevance threshold
    60→        self.relevance_threshold = (
    61→            relevance_threshold
    62→            if relevance_threshold is not None
    63→            else config.RFP_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD
    64→        )
    65→
    66→        self.logger.info(
    67→            "RFPClassifier initialized",
    68→            extra={"relevance_threshold": self.relevance_threshold}
    69→        )
    70→
    71→    @property
    72→    def llm_client(self) -> LLMClient:
    73→        """Get or create the LLM client.
    74→
    75→        Returns:
    76→            LLMClient instance for making classification requests.
    77→        """
    78→        if self._llm_client is None:
    79→            self._llm_client = LLMClient()
    80→        return self._llm_client
    81→
    82→    def classify(self, rfp: RFP) -> ClassificationResult:
    83→        """Classify a single RFP for relevance and extract tags.
    84→
    85→        This method uses Azure OpenAI to analyze the RFP and determine:
    86→        - Relevance score (0.0 to 1.0)
    87→        - Classification tags (AI, Dynamics, Modernization, etc.)
    88→        - Reasoning for the classification
    89→        - Confidence in the classification
    90→
    91→        Args:
    92→            rfp: The RFP model to classify.
    93→
    94→        Returns:
    95→            ClassificationResult with relevance score, tags, and reasoning.
    96→
    97→        Raises:
    98→            ValueError: If classification fails after retries.
    99→        """
   100→        self.logger.info(
   101→            "Classifying RFP",
   102→            extra={"rfp_id": rfp.id, "title": rfp.title[:100]}
   103→        )
   104→
   105→        try:
   106→            # Build additional context from RFP metadata
   107→            additional_context = self._build_context(rfp)
   108→
   109→            # Call LLM for classification
   110→            result = self.llm_client.classify_rfp(
   111→                title=rfp.title,
   112→                description=rfp.description,
   113→                agency=rfp.agency,
   114→                additional_context=additional_context,
   115→            )
   116→
   117→            # Convert raw result to ClassificationResult model
   118→            classification = self._create_classification_result(
   119→                rfp_id=rfp.id,
   120→                raw_result=result,
   121→            )
   122→
   123→            self.logger.info(
   124→                "RFP classified successfully",
   125→                extra={
   126→                    "rfp_id": rfp.id,
   127→                    "relevance_score": classification.relevance_score,
   128→                    "tags": [t.value for t in classification.tags],
   129→                    "is_relevant": classification.is_relevant(self.relevance_threshold),
   130→                }
   131→            )
   132→
   133→            return classification
   134→
   135→        except Exception as e:
   136→            self.logger.error(
   137→                f"Failed to classify RFP: {e}",
   138→                extra={"rfp_id": rfp.id, "title": rfp.title[:50]}
   139→            )
   140→            raise ValueError(f"Classification failed for RFP {rfp.id}: {e}") from e
   141→
   142→    def classify_batch(
   143→        self,
   144→        rfps: List[RFP],
   145→        skip_on_error: bool = True,
   146→    ) -> List[ClassifiedRFP]:
   147→        """Classify a batch of RFPs.
   148→
   149→        This method processes multiple RFPs, optionally skipping failures
   150→        to allow partial batch completion.
   151→
   152→        Args:
   153→            rfps: List of RFP models to classify.
   154→            skip_on_error: If True, continue processing on classification
   155→                          errors. If False, raise on first error.
   156→
   157→        Returns:
   158→            List of ClassifiedRFP objects containing RFPs with their
   159→            classification results.
   160→
   161→        Raises:
   162→            ValueError: If skip_on_error is False and classification fails.
   163→        """
   164→        self.logger.info(
   165→            "Starting batch classification",
   166→            extra={"batch_size": len(rfps)}
   167→        )
   168→
   169→        results: List[ClassifiedRFP] = []
   170→        errors: List[Tuple[str, str]] = []
   171→
   172→        for rfp in rfps:
   173→            try:
   174→                classification = self.classify(rfp)
   175→
   176→                # Update RFP status
   177→                rfp.status = RFPStatus.CLASSIFIED
   178→
   179→                classified_rfp = ClassifiedRFP(
   180→                    rfp=rfp,
   181→                    classification=classification,
   182→                )
   183→                results.append(classified_rfp)
   184→
   185→            except Exception as e:
   186→                error_msg = str(e)
   187→                errors.append((rfp.id, error_msg))
   188→
   189→                self.logger.warning(
   190→                    f"Classification failed for RFP {rfp.id}",
   191→                    extra={"rfp_id": rfp.id, "error": error_msg}
   192→                )
   193→
   194→                if not skip_on_error:
   195→                    raise ValueError(
   196→                        f"Batch classification failed on RFP {rfp.id}: {error_msg}"
   197→                    ) from e
   198→
   199→                # Mark as error but continue
   200→                rfp.status = RFPStatus.ERROR
   201→
   202→        self.logger.info(
   203→            "Batch classification completed",
   204→            extra={
   205→                "total": len(rfps),
   206→                "successful": len(results),
   207→                "errors": len(errors),
   208→            }
   209→        )
   210→
   211→        return results
   212→
   213→    def filter_relevant(
   214→        self,
   215→        classified_rfps: List[ClassifiedRFP],
   216→        threshold: Optional[float] = None,
   217→    ) -> List[ClassifiedRFP]:
   218→        """Filter classified RFPs by relevance threshold.
   219→
   220→        Args:
   221→            classified_rfps: List of ClassifiedRFP objects to filter.
   222→            threshold: Optional threshold override. Defaults to
   223→                      self.relevance_threshold.
   224→
   225→        Returns:
   226→            List of ClassifiedRFP objects that meet the relevance threshold.
   227→        """
   228→        threshold = threshold if threshold is not None else self.relevance_threshold
   229→
   230→        relevant = [
   231→            crfp for crfp in classified_rfps
   232→            if crfp.classification.relevance_score >= threshold
   233→        ]
   234→
   235→        self.logger.info(
   236→            "Filtered by relevance",
   237→            extra={
   238→                "input_count": len(classified_rfps),

entries.detail:
1→# Specification: NAITIVE RFP Radar Deployment
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Deploy the NAITIVE RFP Radar system - an Azure-native automated RFP (Request for Proposal) discovery and proposal generation service. This system scrapes government RFP portals, filters opportunities by age and geography (US-only), uses Azure OpenAI to score relevance and extract tags, stores metadata and proposals in Azure Blob Storage and AI Search, generates Level 3 full proposals in markdown format, and delivers daily digests to a Slack channel (#bots). The goal is to create an "RFP scouting + proposal-writing team that never sleeps."
     6→
     7→## Workflow Type
     8→
     9→**Type**: feature
    10→
    11→**Rationale**: This is a new feature deployment that adds a complete RFP Radar subsystem to the existing Multi-Agent Custom Automation Engine. It involves creating new Python modules, a new Docker container, infrastructure provisioning via Bicep, and integration with external services (Slack, government portals). The scope is significant but well-defined with clear deliverables.
    12→
    13→## Task Scope
    14→
    15→### Services Involved
    16→- **rfp-radar** (primary) - New Python service for RFP scraping, classification, proposal generation, and Slack delivery
    17→- **azure-infrastructure** (integration) - Bicep templates to provision Azure Container Apps Job, Blob Storage container, Search index
    18→- **slack-integration** (integration) - Webhook/bot integration for delivering RFP digests
    19→
    20→### This Task Will:
    21→- [ ] Create new `src/rfp_radar/` Python module with orchestrator, scrapers, classifier, proposal generator, and digest builder
    22→- [ ] Create Dockerfile for RFP Radar containerization
    23→- [ ] Add Bicep modules for RFP Radar Azure infrastructure (Container Apps Job, dedicated blob container)
    24→- [ ] Configure environment variables and secrets for RFP Radar
    25→- [ ] Implement 3 scraper templates (GovTribe, OpenGov, BidNet patterns)
    26→- [ ] Integrate with existing Azure OpenAI deployment for classification
    27→- [ ] Configure Slack bot delivery to #bots channel
    28→- [ ] Add deployment script for RFP Radar service
    29→
    30→### Out of Scope:
    31→- Production scraper endpoint configuration (placeholder URLs provided - real endpoints require portal-specific authentication)
    32→- Slack workspace setup and bot creation (assumes pre-configured bot token)
    33→- Azure subscription provisioning (leverages existing resource group)
    34→- Custom portal scrapers beyond the 3 templates
    35→- Frontend UI for RFP management
    36→
    37→## Service Context
    38→
    39→### RFP Radar Service (New)
    40→
    41→**Tech Stack:**
    42→- Language: Python 3.9+ (required by azure-storage-blob, pydantic v2, requests)
    43→- Framework: Standalone (requests, azure-storage-blob, azure-search-documents, slack_sdk)
    44→- Key directories: `src/rfp_radar/`, `src/rfp_radar/scrapers/`
    45→
    46→**Entry Point:** `src/rfp_radar/main.py`
    47→
    48→**How to Run:**
    49→```bash
    50→# Local development
    51→python -m venv .venv
    52→source .venv/bin/activate
    53→pip install -r src/rfp_radar/requirements.txt
    54→python src/rfp_radar/main.py
    55→
    56→# Docker
    57→docker build -t naitive-rfp-radar:latest -f src/rfp_radar/Dockerfile .
    58→docker run --env-file .env naitive-rfp-radar:latest
    59→```
    60→
    61→**Port:** N/A (batch job, no exposed port)
    62→
    63→### Existing Infrastructure
    64→
    65→**Tech Stack:**
    66→- Infrastructure: Bicep (Azure Resource Manager templates)
    67→- CI/CD: GitHub Actions
    68→- Container Registry: Azure Container Registry (biabcontainerreg.azurecr.io)
    69→

   111→
   112→## Files to Reference
   113→
   114→These files show patterns to follow:
   115→
   116→| File | Pattern to Copy |
   117→|------|----------------|

   123→
   124→## Patterns to Follow
   125→
   126→### Environment Configuration Pattern
   127→
   128→From `src/backend/common/config/app_config.py`:
   129→
   130→```python
   131→class AppConfig:
   132→    def __init__(self):
   133→        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   134→        # Required vs optional pattern
   135→        self.REQUIRED_VAR = self._get_required("VAR_NAME")
   136→        self.OPTIONAL_VAR = self._get_optional("VAR_NAME", "default")
   137→
   138→    def _get_required(self, name: str, default: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
   139→        if name in os.environ:
   140→            return os.environ[name]
   141→        if default is not None:
   142→            return default
   143→        raise ValueError(f"Environment variable {name} not found")
   144→
   145→    def _get_optional(self, name: str, default: str = "") -> str:
   146→        return os.environ.get(name, default)
   147→```
   148→
   149→**Key Points:**
   150→- Use `_get_required()` for mandatory configuration
   151→- Use `_get_optional()` for optional configuration with defaults
   152→- Load via `python-dotenv` for local development
   153→- Cache credentials and clients
   154→
   155→### Azure Credential Pattern
   156→
   157→From `src/backend/common/config/app_config.py`:
   158→
   159→```python
   160→def get_azure_credential(self, client_id=None):
   161→    if self.APP_ENV == "dev":
   162→        return DefaultAzureCredential()
   163→    else:
   164→        return ManagedIdentityCredential(client_id=client_id)
   165→```
   166→
   167→**Key Points:**
   168→- Use `DefaultAzureCredential` for local development
   169→- Use `ManagedIdentityCredential` for production
   170→- Pass `client_id` for user-assigned managed identity
   171→
   172→### Bicep Resource Naming Pattern
   173→
   174→From `infra/main.bicep`:
   175→
   176→```bicep
   177→var solutionSuffix = toLower(trim(replace('${solutionName}${solutionUniqueText}', '-', '')))
   178→var storageAccountName = replace('st${solutionSuffix}', '-', '')
   179→var containerAppResourceName = 'ca-${solutionSuffix}'
   180→```
   181→
   182→**Key Points:**
   183→- Use `solutionSuffix` for unique resource names
   184→- Follow Azure naming conventions (prefixes: st, ca, cae, srch, kv)
   185→- Remove special characters for storage accounts
   186→
   187→## Requirements
   188→
   189→### Functional Requirements
   190→
   191→1. **RFP Portal Scraping**
   192→   - Description: Scrape multiple government RFP portals using extensible scraper framework
   193→   - Acceptance: Successfully fetch RFP listings from at least one portal template
   194→
   195→2. **Age & Geography Filtering**
   196→   - Description: Filter RFPs to US-only, max 3 days old
   197→   - Accep

    31→
    32→
    33→class RFPTag(str, Enum):

    56→    # Dates

    60→    due_date: Optional[datetime] = Field(
    61→        default=None, description="Proposal submission deadline"
    62→    )
    63→    discovered_at: datetime = Field(
    64→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="When we discovered this RFP"
    65→    )
    66→
    67→    # Geography
    68→    location: str = Field(default="", description="Geographic location or region")
    69→    country: str = Field(default="US", description="Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)")
    70→    state: str = Field(default="", description="State or region code")
    71→
    72→    # Documents

    76→    attachments: List[str] = Field(

   100→    @field_validator("country")
   101→    @classmethod
   102→    def validate_country(cls, v: str) -> str:
   103→        """Validate and normalize country code."""
   104→        if v:
   105→            return v.upper()[:2]
   106→        return "US"
   107→
   108→    def is_us_based(self) -> bool:
   109→        """Check if the RFP is US-based."""
   110→        return self.country.upper() == "US"
   111→
   112→    def age_in_days(self) -> int:
   113→        """Calculate the age of the RFP in days from posted_date."""
   114→        if self.posted_date is None:
   115→            return 0
   116→        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.posted_date
   117→        return delta.days
   118→
   119→    def is_within_age_limit(self, max_age_days: int = 3) -> bool:
   120→        """Check if the RFP is within the age limit."""
   121→        return self.age_in_days() <= max_age_days
   122→
   123→
   124→class ClassificationResult(BaseModel):
   125→    """Model representing the AI classification result for an RFP."""
   126→

   143→    confidence: float = Field(

   147→    @field_validator("relevance_score", "confidence")
   148→    @classmethod
   149→    def validate_score(cls, v: float) -> float:
   150→        """Ensure score is within valid range."""
   151→        return max(0.0, min(1.0, v))
   152→
   153→    def is_relevant(self, threshold: float = 0.55) -> bool:
   154→        """Check if the RFP meets the relevance threshold."""
   155→        return self.relevance_score >= threshold
   156→
   157→
   158→class ClassifiedRFP(BaseModel):
   159→    """Model combining an RFP with its classification result."""
   160→
   161→    rfp: RFP = Field(..., description="The RFP data")
   162→    classification: ClassificationResult = Field(
   163→        ..., description="Classification result"
   164→    )
   165→
   166→    def is_actionable(self, relevance_threshold: float = 0.55) -> bool:
   167→        """Check if this classified RFP should be actioned (proposal generated)."""
   168→        return (
   169→            self.rfp.is_us_based()
   170→            and self.classification.is_relevant(relevance_threshold)
   171→        )
   172→
   173→
   174→class ProposalMetadata(BaseModel):

   176→

    23→    """Builder for RFP digest messages.

    32→        max_entries_per_digest: Maximum entries to include in a single digest.

    55→    def __init__(

    57→        brand_name: Optional[str] = None,
